what_it_does: This file is a living projection guide/deliverable record that documents SurfaceNav, a left-edge navigation rail that hosts DNA's navRail design and provides clickable tiles to open the breadth surfaces (decisions, fabric, board, sessions, transcript, and map-home via Escape), with plain fallback buttons if DNA's navRail is not loaded.
observations: Status is marked verified this session: the rail renders and tiles open their surfaces.
It frames the problem as Tim's 'orphan screens' gap — surfaces were built but only opened by window events nothing dispatched.
SurfaceNav is described as a left-edge vertical rail; each tile uses data-nav to delegate opening a surface.
It records a degrade-clean fallback: plain labelled buttons keep navigation working if DNA's navRail is not loaded.
It claims DNA's navRail is rendering but label-only because DNA.iconSVG is currently a no-op.
It separates responsibilities: DNA owns render; the host owns placement plus the functional tile-to-surface link.
It references another board item (board://item-78c63045) and cites commits 0f44693, 5211961 and files src/nav/SurfaceNav.tsx, nav.css.
Open gaps include icons waiting on DNA.iconSVG and the fact that verification was done by projection in verify-mode, not by Tim on his device.
standouts: Verified-by-use claim made and recorded in the same session.
Explicit degrade-clean fallback so the unblock does not depend on DNA's render.
Clear separation of render ownership (DNA) versus placement/functional-link ownership (host).
Self-acknowledged verification gap: not yet confirmed on Tim's device.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses the HARVEST · projection · title convention and board:// URI addressing.
Body uses labeled sections: STATUS, LANE, ABOUT, KIND, SUMMARY, CLAIMS/DECISIONS, RELATIONS, OPEN_QUESTIONS/GAPS, SOURCE_ADDRESS.
References related items through the frontmatter links array with kind 'references' and target board:// URI.
Records source provenance via commit hashes and file paths.
concerns: The verification claim is self-attested by the projection session and explicitly not confirmed by Tim on his device.
Functional correctness depends on an external DNA component (navRail / iconSVG) that is not present in this file.
The file contains no code or symbols, so its claims cannot be independently validated from its contents alone.
notes: A verified projection guide that records how SurfaceNav closes Tim's orphan-screens gap through a left-edge rail with a clean fallback, while flagging that final user-device verification and DNA icons are still pending.
questions: What does 'verify-mode' specifically entail and how is it different from normal use?
What is the exact API contract between DNA's navRail and the host (data-nav attribute, iconSVG behavior, loading mechanism)?
Where are the breadth surfaces themselves defined and what is the complete list?
How does the fallback button implementation differ visually and behaviorally from DNA's rendered tiles?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it documents the SurfaceNav fix and explains how Tim reaches the breadth surfaces.
apparent_themes: Closing orphan-feature gaps by adding reachable entry points.
Graceful degradation when an external design/render dependency is absent.
Separation of rendering responsibility from functional wiring.
Navigation as a linking layer across breadth surfaces.
intention_signals: The file is trying to record that a real user-reachable navigation gap has been fixed, explain the design split so the fix remains robust if DNA's render is unavailable, and capture the remaining verification and cosmetic gaps for handoff.
novelty: The explicit 'orphan-screens' framing and the deliberate degrade-clean fallback that decouples the host's functional navigation link from DNA's render availability, plus the candid limitation that verification was not done on the user's device.
contribution: This file contributes a file-local, verified-at-time-of-writing record of the SurfaceNav deliverable, the rationale for splitting render and host wiring, the degrade-clean fallback strategy, and the remaining verification and icon gaps — information a fused system would keep to understand how breadth surfaces are reached and what still needs validation.
summary_for_embedding: A living projection guide verifying that SurfaceNav adds a left-edge navigation rail with clickable tiles and a fallback if DNA's navRail is missing, so Tim can open previously orphan breadth surfaces including decisions, fabric, board, sessions, transcript, and map-home.
intention_for_embedding: To document and confirm the SurfaceNav fix that links Tim to all built breadth surfaces while keeping the host's navigation wiring independent of DNA's rendering component.
